teaching_card.title: Pronoun Case (Easy)
teaching_card.body: Choose pronoun case by the pronoun's job. Subjects take I, he, she, they, who; objects take me, him, her, them, whom. In compounds, test the pronoun alone to hear the right case.
key_points: In compounds, drop the other name to check.

screens.title: Case follows the job
screens.body: Use subject pronouns (I, he, she, they, who) for subjects and object pronouns (me, him, her, them, whom) for objects. The pronoun's role in the sentence — not how it sounds — decides the case.

screens.title: Two quick tests
screens.body: In a compound ('Maya and I/me'), drop the other person and listen: '…thanked me,' not '…thanked I.' For who vs. whom, answer with he/him: if 'he' fits, use 'who'; if 'him' fits, use 'whom' (both end in m).
key_points: Compound: remove the other name and check.

screens.title: Try the moves
screens.body: 'The coach emailed Noah and (I/me).' Drop 'Noah and': 'emailed me.' 'The athlete (who/whom) the judges praised' — the judges praised HIM, so it's 'whom.'
screens.worked_example: He won -> 'who won'; judges praised him -> 'whom they praised.'
